title: WorkCanvas has finally arrived!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
monday WorkCanvas is out of beta!

After hearing your feedback and implementing some updates, WorkCanvas is now out of beta and we are ready to celebrate!

What is monday workCanvas? It’s kind of like working on a whiteboard, but straight from your monday account and has way more capabilities! It allows you to plan, brainstorm, and visualize any workflow or project in one collaborative whiteboard that syncs directly with your monday.com boards.

Some workCanvas boards:

Mindmap
Brainstorm, organize information, and visualize relationships between different ideas as a first step in bringing ideas to execution.

mindmap2523×1689 327 KB

Org Chart
Visualize your company’s structure, no matter how simple or complex, and store employee data and color by any category.

Org_chart1790×1092 300 KB

SWOT Analysis
Easily identify strengths and weaknesses, as well as external opportunities within your organization for a simple but powerful high-level picture.

SWAT2522×1689 468 KB

Plus, here is a quick tutorial to help you get started:

Try it out and get ready to hear straight from the WorkCanvas team about the different use cases on February 21.

More details coming soon!

posts.author: GuyWorkCanvas (Guy Shachar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

I’m Guy from the WorkCanvas team, and I’d like to address the confusion regarding the pop-up message you might have encountered when trying to get started with WorkCanvas.

We understand that the original message may not have been clear enough and we’re currently working on improving the clarity and conciseness of the pop-up message to ensure a smoother user experience for everyone.

The original intent of the pop-up was to guide monday.com customers with Enterprise packages towards an optimal onboarding path for connecting WorkCanvas to their account. We’ve reached out to each of you to make sure you’re able to get set up ASAP!

If you’re interested in learning more about how to get started with WorkCanvas or have any other questions in mind, please don’t hesitate to reach out directly via PM.

posts.author: sjacques (Sydney Jacques)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, @GuyWorkCanvas! I don’t think I’ve received a message. Could you please send me a message to help me get set up? Thank you!

posts.author: paulgagne (Paul Gagne)
posts.cookedHtml:
WorkCanvas has a lot of intriguing features. I think it’s a nonstarter, however, if we have to opt in every member that we currently have on WorkOS. I can’t see any use cases for some of our departments and therefore don’t need to have them on WorkCanvas (especially not at first).

Options for smaller teams / groups, as for other Monday.com products, within our account would be great. Thanks!

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: I would be also interested for a smaller team since we have to use Miro in some parts due to other reasons i would be happy to use this for some smaller teams that would be more occasional users and would like the integration with Monday boards

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @paulgagne @nealb

WorkCanvas does have great options for smaller teams! Have you checked our the basic version? The basic version is free and great solution for smaller groups who may not need as many capabilities.

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
see the value in the work canvas, but its licensing model doesn’t fit our team’s needs. We’re willing to pay for 5 or 10 members on our premium plan, but not for those who won’t use it, especially when platforms like Miro offer more flexibility.

Competing with giants like Miro seems challenging at your price point. A more adaptable licensing model, allowing teams to select the work canvas for specific members, could be a game-changer.

Take my situation you could be getting 150 usd a month rather than zero.

In contrast we pay for 8 team members on miro for 20 USD a month and have unlimited guests.

What I like about the Monday concept (although unless I upgrade I cannot test it) is the deep integration with Monday.

For that reason it would be great for documentation. Workflow planning and sprint planning on the dev team but as we grow and we need to use miro due to external team connections and established processes I would be not willing to commit to 20, 25, 30 workcanvas licences as the product would grow.

I decided to give you a longer reply as I think that you can do well with this but the pricing model should be aligned to you other products like 3, 5, 10 users at a time. Not all or nothing.

posts.author: paulgagne (Paul Gagne)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,

Thanks for the reply. Unless I’m missing something, it looks like we can’t use the templates that would connect to our Monday account at that level.

Also, another reply from Guy let me know that 25 is the minimum team on WorkCanvas. I can see five or 10 people in our company making use of it, but unfortunately I don’t see any time where we’d have 25.

posts.author: Loco3412 (Lori)
posts.cookedHtml: @GuyWorkCanvas I am also getting the Canvas is not available for your account yet, but will be coming soon! pop up. Could you send me a message to help get me set up?

posts.author: cmorse (Christina Morse)
posts.cookedHtml:
Question after reading some of these comments.

We’re on an Enterprise plan and our account houses multiple departments and subteams of the company. The Marketing Operations team would benefit greatly from using WorkCanvas, but the team is small with only 5-10 members. The Pro plan pricing says $10.90 seat/month and I would assume we could choose which team (or seats) would have access to this feature, then expand to more teams if necessary.

Correct me if I’m wrong, but from the responses below, it seems as though we would have to pay for 25 seats minimum on our Enterprise account even if only one team will be using the features? I could not find the response from Guy confirming this detail, or any information about it on the monday page or WorkCanvas website.

I attempted to sign up so I could trial the features, but am also receiving the message that it isn’t available for our account yet.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone! These are all great questions and feedback for our WorkCanvas team, Guy has been in touch with many of you and if you still have more questions, we are here!

You also have the opportunity to ask questions and learn more about WorkCanvas from Guy and Noam the heads of monday WorkCanvas tomorrow on zoom! It will be a great session about different use cases and features of WorkCanvas…

Register now and get your questions ready!

The power of monday AI (4)1920×1920 116 KB

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Christina,

Just following up and wanted to see if you also checked out the basic version for your team to start testing out the features (its free!). Secondly, in order to add WorkCanvas to your enterprise account you need to be an admin of you account. I will pass along this feedback and get clarity from @GuyWorkCanvas.
